Category: Commodities, Pairs Trading, Arbitrage, Spread Trading
Strategy Description: Exploits the price difference between West Texas Intermediate (WTI) and Brent Crude oil. The spread is mean-reverting because price shocks are typically temporal.
Core Logic: WTI and Brent are chemically similar but differ in production and transportation. The spread moves back to long-term economic equilibrium after deviations.
Entry Conditions: Calculate a 20-day SMA of the WTI/Brent spread. If the current spread is above the SMA, enter a short position in the spread (Short WTI, Long Brent). If below the SMA, enter a long position (Long WTI, Short Brent).
Exit Conditions: Close the short position when the spread crosses below the SMA. Close the long position when the spread crosses above the SMA.
Risk Management Rules: Mean reversion strategy; caution is advised as alpha may deteriorate in out-of-sample periods.
Market Regime Suitability: Unknown; commodities often have negative correlation to equities, potentially providing diversification.
Full Notes: Indicative performance is a weighted average of in-sample and out-of-sample periods. Out-of-sample performance has shown signs of deterioration.
Mathematical/Statistical Concepts: Mean reversion, moving averages, cointegration, ARMA models, Hidden Markov Models.
